channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: pgvector vs Dedicated Vector Databases: An Honest Comparison
item.description: pgvector is genuinely good. Dedicated vector databases exist for real reasons. Here's how to think through the tradeoff without the vendor hype.

item.title: The Four Types of Memory in AI Agents (And Why Most Systems Only Use One)
item.description: Sensory, short-term, episodic, semantic: AI agents can use all four types of memory. Nearly every implementation uses only the context window and then wonders why long tasks fall apart.

item.title: HNSW: What's Actually Happening When You Do a Vector Search
item.description: Vector database users often treat HNSW as a black box. Here's the actual algorithm, the tradeoffs, and when you should care.

item.title: Your Context Window Has a Middle. Models Don't Read It.
item.description: The 'lost in the middle' problem is real, quantified, and not solved by bigger context windows. Here's what the research actually says about where LLMs pay attention.

item.title: Why AI Agent Testing Is Harder Than It Looks
item.description: AI agents break in ways that unit tests don't catch. Here's a systematic approach to testing non-deterministic systems that actually ship.

item.title: AI Agents Keep Failing in Production. Here's Why.
item.description: 95% of enterprise AI systems fail to reach production. The ones that do mostly fail within six months. The reasons aren't mysterious.
